# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **Arrays as Function Arguments**
+ **Bubble Sort**
+ **Processing Array Contents**
:::

::: notes
Week 11 second lecture. Students passed arrays to functions (decay, size parameter), sorted with bubble sort, and computed sums/min/max. This lecture introduces std::array, the three-type comparison, multi-dimensional arrays, and the stack lab.
:::

---

# Today's Agenda

- `std::array`
- Comparing all three types
- Passing to functions
- Parallel arrays
- Two-dimensional arrays
- Three or more dimensions *(optional)*
- Lab — array-backed stack

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_std_array.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_three_types.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_passing_functions.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_4_parallel_arrays.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_5_two_dimensional.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_6_three_dimensions.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_7_stack_lab.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- `std::array<T,N>` is a fixed-size array with `.size()`, `.at()`, and no pointer decay on function calls
- Default to `vector`; use `std::array` for fixed-size collections; use `int[]` only for C interop
- 2D arrays are declared `type name[rows][cols]` and traversed with nested loops — outer on rows, inner on columns
- A stack wraps an array with a `top` index and a five-function API; push/pop modify `top` by reference

---

# Next Time

---

# Appendix

---

## std::array: Good Practices

---

## std::array: Common Mistakes

---

## std::array: More Examples

---

## std::array: Practice Problems

---

## std::array: AI Search Terms

---

## std::array: Questions for Reflection

---

## Comparing All Three Types: Good Practices

---

## Comparing All Three Types: Common Mistakes

---

## Comparing All Three Types: More Examples

---

## Comparing All Three Types: Practice Problems

---

## Comparing All Three Types: AI Search Terms

---

## Comparing All Three Types: Questions for Reflection

---

## Passing to Functions: Good Practices

---

## Passing to Functions: Common Mistakes

---

## Passing to Functions: More Examples

---

## Passing to Functions: Practice Problems

---

## Passing to Functions: AI Search Terms

---

## Passing to Functions: Questions for Reflection

---

## Parallel Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Parallel Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Parallel Arrays: More Examples

---

## Parallel Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Parallel Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Parallel Arrays: Questions for Reflection

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: More Examples

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Questions for Reflection

---

## Three or More Dimensions: Good Practices

---

## Three or More Dimensions: Common Mistakes

---

## Three or More Dimensions: More Examples

---

## Three or More Dimensions: Practice Problems

---

## Three or More Dimensions: AI Search Terms

---

## Three or More Dimensions: Questions for Reflection

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Good Practices

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Common Mistakes

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: More Examples

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Practice Problems

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: AI Search Terms

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Questions for Reflection
